9f6fb34b357dc6fd.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
9f6fb34b357dc6fd.summary: OpenAI completed the acquisition of TBPN — the founder-focused business talk show network — as it expands its media and content distribution play. No financial terms disclosed. TBPN's audience of startup founders and VCs will now get OpenAI-first coverage.

9c122a9af19cc98b.title: Microsoft launches 3 new AI models in direct shot at OpenAI and Google
9c122a9af19cc98b.summary: Microsoft's newly formed superintelligence team, led by Mustafa Suleyman, dropped three models simultaneously: MAI-Transcribe-1 (speech-to-text), MAI-Voice-1 (human-like voice synthesis), and MAI-Image-2 (image generation). Available immediately via Microsoft Foundry and the new MAI Playground. A clear signal that Microsoft is building AI self-sufficiency beyond OpenAI.

bbe32fb8c70a7d49.title: Google releases Gemma 4 under Apache 2.0 — and that license change may matter more than benchmarks
bbe32fb8c70a7d49.summary: While Alibaba's Qwen has been restricting open releases of its latest models, Google went the opposite direction: Gemma 4 ships under Apache 2.0 — fully open for commercial use with no restrictions. The strategic intent: position Google as the 'open' alternative and undercut the closed-model ecosystem. Benchmark numbers matter less than the licensing signal.

492531e48f697148.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
492531e48f697148.summary: Google's Vids app gets prompt-driven avatar direction — users can now tell AI avatars what to do and say via natural language prompts. Part of Google's broader AI video push to compete with OpenAI's (now shut down) Sora and Runway.

23578f1fbb7c02d1.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code
23578f1fbb7c02d1.summary: In what appears to be an overzealous DMCA-style enforcement sweep, Anthropic sent takedown requests to thousands of GitHub repositories. The trigger: Claude Code source code had leaked online. Anthropic acknowledged the move was accidental — many repos had nothing to do with the leak. Raises serious questions about automated enforcement at scale.

696c8c3bc113505e.title: Claude Code's source code appears to have leaked: here's what we know
696c8c3bc113505e.summary: Anthropic's AI coding agent, Claude Code, had its source code published online — giving competitors a literal blueprint for building a high-agency, commercially viable coding agent. Cursor, Copilot, and others now have an architectural reference. The leak was live for several days before being pulled.

665ffbd46541b903.title: Anthropic is having a month
665ffbd46541b903.summary: Claude's paid consumer adoption is 'skyrocketing' per multiple signals — the combination of the leak incident, GitHub takedown drama, and the Claude 3.5 rollout has kept Anthropic at the center of AI conversation all month.

851f258cdd382e62.title: OpenAI, not yet public, raises $3B from retail investors in monster $122B fund raise
851f258cdd382e62.summary: OpenAI closed $3B from retail investors in a $122B fundraising round — the largest retail raise in AI history. Despite not being public, OpenAI opened the round to ordinary investors through Wealthfront and similar platforms. $122B valuation makes it the most valuable private AI company in the world.

417cba462c132595.title: Why OpenAI really shut down Sora
417cba462c132595.summary: After Sora's launch, OpenAI pulled the product. The real reasons appear to be a combination of compute costs, legal liability from generated content, and internal competition from Veo and other video models. A reality check moment for the entire AI video category.

9c74672117844f56.title: Elon Musk's last co-founder reportedly leaves xAI
9c74672117844f56.summary: The last remaining xAI co-founder other than Musk has left the company. xAI is now effectively Musk alone. The departure accelerates questions about xAI's technical depth and long-term team stability as it tries to compete with OpenAI and Google DeepMind.

ac412cb38932b2d2.title: Mistral AI raises $830M in debt to set up a data center near Paris
ac412cb38932b2d2.summary: Europe's flagship AI lab is building physical infrastructure: $830M in debt financing to construct a dedicated data center near Paris. The move signals Mistral's transition from model API business to full-stack AI company with proprietary compute — and a direct challenge to US cloud providers for European enterprise workloads.

4d7a2758a1f478a3.title: Meta's natural gas binge could power South Dakota
4d7a2758a1f478a3.summary: A deep investigation into Meta's energy consumption reveals its AI data centers are burning natural gas at a scale that could power an entire US state. The environmental cost of training and running frontier models is becoming a serious reputational and regulatory risk for big tech.

1e9baeba5a1f162a.title: AI chip startup Rebellions raises $400 million at $2.3B valuation in pre-IPO round
1e9baeba5a1f162a.summary: South Korea's Rebellions raised $400M at a $2.3B valuation in a pre-IPO round. The company designs custom AI inference chips — ATOM, its NPU — targeting data center efficiency. The IPO is expected in H2 2026. Backers include KDB Investment and other sovereign funds.

1d29eda41cc3558f.title: Starcloud raises $170 million Series A to build data centers in space
1d29eda41cc3558f.summary: Starcloud raised $170M Series A to deploy AI-optimized data centers in low Earth orbit. The company argues that space-based compute can eliminate land costs, cooling requirements, and achieve very low latency via direct-to-earth optical links. Ambitious and speculative — but the funding came from serious space VCs.

3e7c0a897d02fa02.title: ScaleOps raises $130M to improve computing efficiency amid AI demand
3e7c0a897d02fa02.summary: ScaleOps — which automates cloud resource optimization for AI workloads — raised $130M Series C. As enterprises run more GPU-intensive workloads, efficient compute allocation is becoming a first-order priority and a large market opportunity.

110c4ade92aa9a08.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
110c4ade92aa9a08.summary: Cognichip raised $60M to use AI in the chip design process itself — automating RTL generation, floor planning, and timing closure. If it works, it could compress chip design cycles from years to months. A meta-AI play in the hardware stack.

d88012e6f649244a.title: Nvidia-backed ThinkLabs AI raises $28M to tackle a growing power grid crunch
d88012e6f649244a.summary: ThinkLabs AI uses physics-informed neural networks to model electrical grid behavior in real time, compressing engineering studies from weeks to minutes. The $28M raise was led by Nvidia's venture arm. Critical infrastructure AI that doesn't get as much attention as generative AI but may be more consequential.

dc0bf1a6d7a22914.title: Runway launches $10M fund, Builders program to support early-stage AI startups
dc0bf1a6d7a22914.summary: Runway, facing increasing competition from OpenAI (Sora), Google (Veo), and Pika, is investing in the startup ecosystem to build competitive moats. The Builders program offers GPU credits, mentorship, and distribution partnerships for early-stage AI video and media companies.

afc419990655d72a.title: Intuit's AI agents hit 85% repeat usage. The secret was keeping humans involved
afc419990655d72a.summary: After shipping AI to 3 million customers, Intuit found the key to retention wasn't better chatbots — it was knowing exactly when to hand off to a human expert. 85% repeat usage is an exceptional number for AI consumer products. The insight: hybrid human-AI workflows outperform pure automation for complex financial tasks.

cb28e4e1b676ef56.title: Slack adds 30 AI features to Slackbot, its most ambitious update since the Salesforce acquisition
cb28e4e1b676ef56.summary: Salesforce delivered its most significant Slack overhaul: 30 new AI features across summarization, search, workflow automation, and data retrieval. Inside Salesforce, teams claim 20 hours per week savings per employee — worth $6.4M+ in productivity. On track to be the fastest-adopted product in Salesforce's 27-year history.

129e3643a5bf7a62.title: Salesforce announces an AI-heavy makeover for Slack, with 30 new features
129e3643a5bf7a62.summary: Concurrent coverage confirming Slack's AI transformation. The features lean heavily into enterprise workflow automation — connecting to Salesforce CRM data, automating ticket routing, and providing real-time meeting summaries.

c968662662795b62.title: The end of 'shadow AI' at enterprises? Kilo launches KiloClaw for Organizations
c968662662795b62.summary: Shadow AI — employees using unsanctioned AI tools at work — is the enterprise IT nightmare of 2026. KiloClaw for Organizations introduces 'bot accounts' for every employee (e.g., scott.bot@kiloco.ai), giving IT departments visibility and control over AI tool usage while letting workers keep their personal AI workflows.

3fbadfb20c19a36d.title: Meta's new structured prompting technique makes LLMs significantly better at code review
3fbadfb20c19a36d.summary: Meta published a prompting technique that boosts LLM code review accuracy to 93% in some cases — no fine-tuning, no extra tools, no training. The method is execution-free and can be applied out-of-the-box to any LLM via structured output formats. A significant productivity unlock for development teams.

da3cfb09fde4c382.title: Cohere's open-weight ASR model hits 5.4% word error rate — low enough to replace speech APIs
da3cfb09fde4c382.summary: Cohere released an open-weight automatic speech recognition (ASR) model with a 5.4% WER — comparable to Whisper and well within production-quality thresholds. The open-weight release means enterprises can run transcription entirely on-premises with no API costs or data leaving their infrastructure.

f0e16a0b9203fec0.title: Softr launches AI-native platform to help nontechnical teams build business apps without code
f0e16a0b9203fec0.summary: Softr's AI Co-Builder lets non-technical users describe apps in plain language and generates a fully integrated system: database, UI, permissions, and business logic — connected and ready for deployment. The next evolution of no-code: AI that understands intent and builds the full stack.

fde7f43b4d640239.title: When product managers ship code: AI just broke the software org chart
fde7f43b4d640239.summary: A product manager at a software company built, tested, and shipped a feature to production in a single day using AI tools. No handoff to engineering, no ticket created. The traditional software development hierarchy is being compressed by AI coding tools — this is a first-person account of what it looks like in practice.

a1de9e281ddeb30d.title: Bluesky leans into AI with Attie, an app for building custom feeds
a1de9e281ddeb30d.summary: Bluesky launched Attie — an AI app for building custom content feeds on the Bluesky social network. The social platform is betting that AI-powered personalization, built on an open protocol, can compete with the algorithmic feeds of X and Threads.

52e11b8667a76582.title: Alexa+ gets new food ordering experiences with Uber Eats and Grubhub
52e11b8667a76582.summary: Amazon's Alexa Plus integration with Uber Eats and Grubhub puts food ordering inside the voice AI assistant experience. The move signals Amazon's push to make Alexa Plus a daily utility — not just a smart speaker novelty.

10a8be8fc891fe53.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
10a8be8fc891fe53.summary: AI hiring platform Mercor disclosed a cyberattack traced to a vulnerability in the open-source LiteLLM project — a popular AI gateway library used by thousands of companies to manage LLM API calls. The compromise of LiteLLM is a supply-chain level event affecting numerous AI companies simultaneously.

99315ec461e3371d.title: Popular AI gateway startup LiteLLM ditches controversial startup Delve
99315ec461e3371d.summary: LiteLLM — the AI gateway startup whose compromised dependency triggered the Mercor breach — dropped its integration with Delve, an AI startup facing scrutiny for its surveillance practices targeting HR and recruiting. The dual pressure of a security incident and ethical concerns forced the split.

70262b8641caf133.title: Stanford study outlines dangers of asking AI chatbots for personal advice
70262b8641caf133.summary: A Stanford study catalogs the risks of using AI chatbots for personal, financial, and health advice: hallucinated citations, emotionally manipulative outputs, and lack of accountability. As AI assistants become primary advice sources for millions, the liability questions are becoming urgent.

dcf9e2d5a528faf5.title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers
dcf9e2d5a528faf5.summary: A new benchmark for evaluating AI agents performing real-world computer tasks — browsing, filing, writing emails, using desktop applications. From Zhe Yang, Shuai Liu, Ziwei Liu et al. (SCV-NTU). Tests agents on actual PC workflows, not synthetic environments. Project page: hippocamp-ai.github.io

9b62a9d401d3a7bc.title: OmniMem: Autoresearch-Guided Discovery of Lifelong Multimodal Agent Memory
9b62a9d401d3a7bc.summary: An agent memory system that autonomously discovers and retains useful knowledge across long horizons. OmniMem uses an auto-research loop to identify what to remember, compressing multimodal experiences into reusable knowledge structures. From Huaxiu Yao, Cihang Xie et al.

c770bca470db48e4.title: PsychAgent: An Experience-Driven Lifelong Learning Agent for Self-Evolving Psychological Counselor
c770bca470db48e4.summary: A lifelong learning agent designed as a psychological counselor — it retains session history, updates its approach based on interaction outcomes, and maintains therapeutic context across long conversations. From Yutao Yang, Junsong Li, Liang He et al. Strong signal that AI mental health support is moving from chatbot novelty to clinical-grade self-evolving systems.

79760d0d90be3cbc.title: RefineRL: Advancing Competitive Programming with Self-Refinement Reinforcement Learning
79760d0d90be3cbc.summary: RefineRL applies reinforcement learning with self-refinement to competitive programming. The agent generates code solutions, executes them against test cases, uses feedback to refine — iteratively. From Microsoft Research (Shaopeng Fu, Xingxing Zhang, Furu Wei). State-of-the-art potential on competitive programming benchmarks.

85a075173414f0c6.title: The Silicon Mirror: Dynamic Behavioral Gating for Anti-Sycophancy in LLM Agents
85a075173414f0c6.summary: Sycophancy — LLMs agreeing with users even when wrong — is especially dangerous in agentic contexts. The Silicon Mirror introduces a gating mechanism that detects when the agent is shaping its response to please rather than being accurate. Code and evaluation data available on GitHub. Independent researcher Harshee Jignesh Shah.

d00fb5ecf430ad66.title: Detecting Multi-Agent Collusion Through Multi-Agent Interpretability
d00fb5ecf430ad66.summary: A safety research paper addressing multi-agent systems where multiple AI agents may coordinate in ways that are hard to interpret or detect. The paper introduces interpretability tools for identifying emergent collusive behavior in agent networks. From Oxford Future of Humanity Institute researchers.

827efd744bc935e3.title: Adversarial Moral Stress Testing of Large Language Models
827efd744bc935e3.summary: A systematic stress-testing framework for LLM moral reasoning under adversarial conditions. Tests models against systematically constructed moral dilemmas designed to trigger inconsistent, harmful, or contradictory outputs. From Polytechnique Montréal and McGill researchers.

515fe180297bc4d6.title: Agent psychometrics: Task-level performance prediction in agentic coding benchmarks
515fe180297bc4d6.summary: Rather than benchmarking agent performance on aggregate scores, Agent Psychometrics introduces task-level predictive models — predicting which specific coding tasks an agent will fail on, before running them. From Daniel Fried, Uzay Girit (allenai). A fundamental advance in agent evaluation methodology.

d577add0dba9fbfd.title: YC-Bench: Benchmarking AI Agents for Long-Term Planning and Consistent Execution
d577add0dba9fbfd.summary: Yet Another Coding benchmark — YC-Bench tests agents not just on individual coding tasks but on multi-step projects requiring consistent execution over days of simulated work. Tests for drift, task abandonment, and compounding errors. From Invoke AI and Allen Institute for AI.

c741b378d84df719.title: BloClaw: An Omniscient, Multi-Modal Agentic Workspace for Next-Generation Scientific Discovery
c741b378d84df719.summary: An AI workspace that simultaneously sees your code, documents, terminal, browser, and data — and can take actions across all of them to advance scientific research goals. BloClaw is being positioned as a scientific co-researcher. From Yao Qin (UCSB), Jinhua Pang.

248d830208a1a887.title: UK AISI Alignment Evaluation Case-Study
248d830208a1a887.summary: The UK's AI Safety Institute published its first major alignment evaluation — an in-depth case study of frontier model behavior under controlled conditions. Authors include Robert Kirk (UK AISI) and Abby D'Cruz. The most transparent view yet into how a major government's AI safety body actually evaluates models.

26fb19205c7a1e79.title: 15% of Americans say they'd be willing to work for an AI boss
26fb19205c7a1e79.summary: A Quinnipiac poll found 15% of Americans would be willing to work under an AI supervisor — and 85% would not. The generational divide is stark. Among 18-34 year olds, openness to AI management is significantly higher. As AI agents enter workplace management roles, this data will shape enterprise adoption strategies.

722e2883c93d8a0d.title: As more Americans adopt AI tools, fewer say they can trust the results
722e2883c93d8a0d.summary: A new survey shows AI adoption rising while trust in AI outputs falls — the 'trust gap' is widening. Americans are using AI more but believing it less. A critical challenge for AI companies as deployment scales.

d341d6aa319e76f5.title: Midjourney engineer debuts new vibe coded, open source standard Pretext to revolutionize web design
d341d6aa319e76f5.summary: A Midjourney engineer created 'Pretext' — an open-source standard for 'vibe coding' web layouts, where text is treated as a fluid substance that can be recalculated every single frame. A playful but technically serious re-imagination of how developers interact with UI layout at the material level.

8e903737934971b4.title: Qodo raises $70M for code verification as AI coding scales
8e903737934971b4.summary: Qodo (formerly Melos) raised $70M for its code verification platform — products that check AI-generated code for correctness, security, and style compliance. As AI-generated code proliferates, the verification layer becomes as important as the generation layer.

9f86ce848c153496.title: Yupp shuts down after raising $33M from a16z crypto's Chris Dixon
9f86ce848c153496.summary: Yupp, an AI-powered startup backed by a16z crypto and Chris Dixon, shut down. $33M raised, no product-market fit. Another data point in the growing list of well-funded AI startups that couldn't find a sustainable business model.

505e08cf76a4e66d.title: Nomadic raises $8.4M to wrangle the data pouring off autonomous vehicles
505e08cf76a4e66d.summary: Nomadic raised $8.4M to handle the massive data management challenge of autonomous vehicle fleets — annotation, storage, retrieval, and real-time processing of sensor data. Boring infrastructure play that becomes critical as Waymo, Tesla, and others scale their AV fleets.
